description: 30-item personality questionnaire covering Big Five (OCEAN) and MBTI dimensions. Items drawn from or modelled on the IPIP (International Personality Item Pool, public domain). Presented in randomised order per user.

items.text: I enjoy thinking about abstract ideas and theories, even when they don't have an obvious practical use.
I tend to notice beauty in things that other people might overlook — a well-designed object, an interesting turn of phrase, a striking visual.
I generally prefer to stick with approaches that have worked before rather than trying unfamiliar methods.
I often find myself lost in thought, exploring ideas just for the pleasure of it.
I like to have a clear plan before I start a task — jumping in without one makes me uncomfortable.
I sometimes let things slide when I should be more disciplined about following through.
I pay close attention to details and find it hard to let small errors go uncorrected.
I tend to do things in a methodical, step-by-step way rather than improvising as I go.
After an intense day of meetings and social interaction, I need quiet time alone to recharge.
In group settings, I naturally take on an active role — I find it easy to speak up and engage.
I find being around people energising rather than draining, even after a long day.
I prefer one-on-one conversations to large group discussions.
I tend to trust that people generally have good intentions until shown otherwise.
In a disagreement, I'd rather be straightforwardly honest than carefully diplomatic.
It genuinely upsets me when I see someone being treated unfairly, even if it doesn't affect me personally.
I find it difficult to say no when someone asks for my help, even when I'm overloaded.
I tend to worry about things that might go wrong, even when everything seems fine.
I recover quickly from setbacks — when something goes wrong, I don't dwell on it for long.
Criticism stings more than I'd like to admit, and I sometimes replay critical feedback in my mind.
I often feel calm and steady under pressure, even when things around me are chaotic.
I think best when I can talk things through with someone else rather than working them out on my own.
I tend to reflect carefully before sharing my thoughts, rather than thinking out loud.
I often notice patterns and connections between things that don't seem obviously related.
I'd rather work with concrete facts and real-world data than hypothetical possibilities.
When someone explains something to me, I'm more interested in the big picture and implications than the specific details.
When making a difficult decision, I tend to weigh the logical pros and cons more than how it will affect people's feelings.
When a friend is upset, my instinct is to listen and empathise rather than to offer solutions.
I find it more important for a decision to be fair and consistent than for everyone involved to be happy with the outcome.
I feel more comfortable when things are decided and settled rather than left open-ended.
I prefer to keep my options open as long as possible rather than committing to a plan early.
